steps.title: Understand the structure
steps.content: The first sentence lists Copeland's accomplishments (Broadway, touring with Prince, President's Council). The blank introduces a sentence saying "nothing in her career matches" one specific honor. That pivot — from many achievements to a single supreme one — requires a contrast signal.

steps.title: Eliminate each choice
steps.content:
• Choice A — Thus,: Signals a logical result or conclusion from what came before. The next sentence is not a result of her accomplishments; it contrasts with them. ✗
  • Choice B — However,: Signals contrast or exception — exactly right. It acknowledges the prior accomplishments while pivoting to the claim that one honor surpasses them all. ✓
  • Choice C — For example,: Signals that what follows illustrates the previous point. Being named principal dancer is not another example of her accomplishments; it's set apart from them. ✗
  • Choice D — Second of all,: Signals a second item in a list. The sentence is not a second accomplishment — it explicitly says nothing else matches this honor. ✗
That is the correct answer.
